subject: Project Management
title: Value Stream Mapping
description: A 60-second lesson on Value Stream Mapping.

hook.text: A value stream map draws every step in a process — and reveals how much of it adds no value.

buildup.text: Steps are classified as value-adding, non-value-adding but necessary, or pure waste.

discovery.text: Typically, only 5-10% of steps in a process actually add value from the customer's perspective.

twist.text: The map makes waste undeniable. Managers can't argue with a visual showing 90% waste.

climax.text: Value stream mapping is lean's most powerful diagnostic tool — a process X-ray that shows everything.

punchline.text: Draw the process. The waste draws itself.

quiz.question: What does value stream mapping typically reveal?
options: Only 5-10% of process steps actually add customer value
